description: You are a Solar Performance Engineer evaluating a utility-scale solar farm layout for a new site in Phoenix, Arizona. The extreme summer temperatures will cause massive voltage drops and power degradation in standard solar panels. The Energy3D project 'solar-rack-array.ng3' is open. You must: 1. Change the project location/city to Phoenix, AZ. 2. Select all solar panels in the array and update their physical properties to match a premium HJT module: Cell Efficiency to 22% (or 0.22), Temperature Coefficient of Pmax to -0.26 %/°C, and Nominal Operating Cell Temperature (NOCT) to 43°C. 3. Run an Annual Yield Analysis (or Annual Solar Radiation Analysis) to simulate the total energy production of the upgraded array. 4. Save the modified project as 'hjt_upgrade_phoenix.ng3' in the ~/Documents/Energy3D/ directory. 5. Create a text file at '~/Documents/Energy3D/yield_report.txt' containing the new total annual yield of the array in kWh (e.g., 'Annual Yield: 125000 kWh').
